story-context.title: User Validation and Injection Detection (Layers 9-10)
story-context.summary: Implement JWT Layers 9-10: Optional user validation (check user exists and is active) and comprehensive injection detection (SQL, XSS, JNDI, Expression Injection, Path Traversal). This story completes the 10-layer JWT validation defense-in-depth strategy, providing OWASP A03 (Injection) protection and optional user existence validation.

acceptance-criteria.criterion: Layer 9 (optional): User validation - check user exists and is active (configurable)
Layer 10: Injection detection for SQL, XSS, JNDI, Expression Injection, Path Traversal patterns
Injection patterns detected and rejected with 400 Bad Request
Fuzz test with Jazzer targets injection detection (SQL/XSS/Expression/PathTraversal payloads)
Performance impact <5ms per request (Layer 9 + Layer 10 combined)
Layer 9 user validation can be disabled via configuration for performance

critical-security-fixes.alert: CRITICAL FIX APPLIED: Added Expression Injection patterns (\${...}) for JNDI/SpEL injection detection
CRITICAL FIX APPLIED: Added Path Traversal patterns (../) for directory traversal prevention
CRITICAL FIX APPLIED: InjectionDetectedException extends EafException (not RuntimeException)
PERFORMANCE FIX: Regex patterns moved to companion object (compile once)

sections.section: Lines 419-491: InjectionDetector.kt CORRECTED with all 5 pattern categories

example.name: InjectionDetector.kt
example.description: CRITICAL: Comprehensive injection detection with 5 pattern categories

example.name: JwtFormatFuzzer.kt

implementation-sequence.step: Configure application.yml with Redis properties and revocation.fail-closed
Create InjectionDetector.kt with companion object for patterns
Implement all 5 pattern categories (SQL, XSS, JNDI, Expression, PathTraversal)
Create RedisRevocationStore.kt with @Value injection for fail-closed
Write integration tests for both fail-open and fail-closed modes
Write fuzz test for injection detection (Jazzer)
Configure Micrometer metrics for revocation operations

unit-tests.test: InjectionDetector allows safe claim values (no false positives)

integration-tests.test: Revoke token → subsequent request with same JWT → 401
Revoke token → different JWT → 200 OK (not affected)

fuzz-tests.goal: Find bypasses, DoS conditions, false positives

performance-targets.target: Combined: <5ms per request (Layer 9 disabled by default)

common-pitfalls.pitfall: SQL pattern too broad (flagging "O'Malley" as injection) - refined in fix
Missing Expression Injection patterns (${...}) - CRITICAL vulnerability
Missing Path Traversal patterns (../) - CRITICAL vulnerability
Using RuntimeException instead of EafException - coding standards violation
Regex compiled per instance instead of companion object - performance issue

definition-of-done.item: InjectionDetector with all 5 pattern categories (SQL, XSS, JNDI, Expression, PathTraversal)
InjectionDetectedException extends EafException with structured fields
Integration tests pass for both fail-open and fail-closed modes
Fuzz test runs 5 minutes without crashes
